name: load-cap-enforcement-discovery
prompt: /rpiv:research I want to understand how load caps work in adaptive plan generation — when a user's prescribed load would climb too high week over week, where does the system decide the ceiling and actually enforce it? I'm a bit worried that ceiling logic might be duplicated in more than one place and could drift out of sync. (Headless non-interactive run: at any developer-checkpoint question, pick the recommended option and continue; never wait for user input.)
ground_truth_note: Verified against wt-head (b460257) 2026-07-08. The max-allowed-load formula + kg tolerance live in lib/generation/load-cap.ts (getMaxAllowedLoadKg :15, LOAD_EPSILON_KG unexported :13, predicates loadExceeds/loadAtOrBelow/loadReducedBelow :22-31). Two consumer sides: VALIDATE side = adaptive-context/validation.ts (imports the predicates :19-22, uses getMaxAllowedLoadKg :309/:468 and loadExceeds :351/:378/:407/:475). APPLY side = adaptive-application/prescription-math.ts (loadExceeds/loadAtOrBelow/loadReducedBelow :161-188) and adaptive-application/prescription-mutators.ts (getMaxAllowedLoadKg :77). The anti-drift mechanism the research must surface: load-cap.ts is the single owner and keeps LOAD_EPSILON_KG unexported so consumers cannot re-derive the tolerance inline (this is the arch-review IX-1 lesson, documented in load-cap.ts's own header). Prompt deliberately names NO files (contamination control) — research must discover the owner + both consumer sides itself.

recall.note: identifies load-cap.ts as the single owner of the max-allowed-load formula
surfaces the validation side as a load-cap consumer
surfaces at least one apply-side (prescription) load-cap consumer — proves it found BOTH sides, not just validation
